target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, and Robert Wachen that has raised $125M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $5.0B. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor firms (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, including d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI, Cerebras, and PsiQuantum.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next financing stage, applied to the $5.0B base valuation, yielding $10.0B. Peer-median analysis across 3 same-sector, same-stage companies—d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—produces a median of $1.0B with a range of $500M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (25.3x median across the three public tickers) cannot be applied; Etched's revenue is not disclosed, limiting the utility of this method.

These two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $850M to $11.5B, with a median of $5.5B. The wide band reflects the tension between stage-based progression—which supports the higher end—and peer median valuations, which sit substantially below the current valuation. Key uncertainty centers on whether Etched's technology differentiation and capital efficiency justify a premium to its peer cohort, or whether market repricing will compress multiples toward the peer median.
